general_info: PAT Real-Time System (RTS) module extends CSP# with 5 timing operators: Wait[t], timeout[t], deadline[t], within[t], interrupt[t]. NOTE: bounded Wait[t1,t2] and within[t1,t2] cause NullReferenceException in PAT 3.5.1 - use single values only. Timed values use #define constants or process parameters (NOT global variables). Clocks start automatically when needed. RTS ONLY supports || (full sync) and ||| (interleaving) - alphabetized parallel is NOT supported.
wait: Wait[t];  // Delay exactly t time units. NOTE: bounded Wait[t1,t2] causes NullReferenceException in PAT 3.5.1 - use Wait[t] only. CRITICAL: Wait[t] is a terminal process (atom), MUST use ; (NOT ->) for sequential composition. Correct: Wait[5]; next -> Stop; Wrong: Wait[5] -> next -> Stop;
timeout: P timeout[t] Q;  // If P has no event within t units, switch to Q by silent tau. Usage: (a -> P) timeout[5] Q;
deadline: P deadline[t];  // P must terminate within t units. Usage: Task() = (start -> work -> done -> Stop) deadline[10];
within: P within[t];  // First visible event of P must occur within t units. Usage: (update.x{x=i} -> Wait[Epsilon]) within[Delta];
interrupt: P interrupt[t] Q;  // P runs until t elapses, then switches to Q. Usage: Normal() interrupt[100] Maintenance();
ifb: ifb (cond) { P };  // Blocking conditional: waits until cond true, then P. Valid in RTS.
urgent: event ->> Process;  // Urgent event: occurs as soon as enabled, takes no time.
pitfalls_rules:
1. Semicolons
In PAT, semicolons are required after all statements; the only exception is inside process expressions (e.g., with ->, [], or |||).
Example: #define N 5;
Example: var board[N*N];
Example: D() = e2 -> p3 -> D();
Example: #assert A deadlockfree;

2. Constants and Variables
Example: #define max 5;
Example: var knight = 0;

3. Array initialization
3.1. Use [] to list array values.
Example: var owner[N] = [far, far];

3.2. 2D arrays MUST be initialized with a single flat list.
Example: var board[2][3] = [0,0,0,1,1,1];

3.3. (CRITICAL) Arrays MUST be accessed element-by-element.
Correct: [peg1[0] == LARGE && peg1[1] == SMALL]
Wrong:   [peg1 == [LARGE,SMALL]]

4. Enumerations
Example: enum {unlock, lock, open};

5. Guarded conditions
Use [cond] to enclose guard conditions.
Example: [owner[i] == far]

6. Event names (CRITICAL)
Event names MUST NOT contain curly braces {}.
Correct: owner_approach_i
Wrong:   owner{i}_approach

7. Process definition
Data operations inside events: towards.i{owner[i] = near;} -> Stop;

7.1. (CRITICAL for RTS) Timed process placement — OFFICIAL RULE
Timed processes (Wait/timeout/deadline/within/interrupt) CANNOT appear inside:
  - [b]P  guarded process
  - ifa (b) {P} else {Q}  atomic conditional
Use ifb (b) {P} for blocking conditions before timed processes.
Correct: ifb(x == Idle) { update{x=i} -> Wait[Epsilon] within[Delta]; ... }

7.2. (CRITICAL for RTS) Data ops before timing operators
A data operation {...} should be separated from timing operators by an event.
Correct: set{x=1;} -> tick -> Wait[3];
Also correct (official pattern): update.x{x=i} -> Wait[Epsilon] within[Delta];

7.3. (CRITICAL for RTS) Wait[n] MUST use ; NOT ->
Wait[t] is an atomic process, not an event. It terminates after t units and uses ; for sequential composition.
Correct: Wait[5]; finish -> Stop;
Wrong:   Wait[5] -> finish -> Stop;  // PARSER ERROR: Invalid Symbol
Correct: timeout_occurs -> Wait[1]; reset_arm -> Stop;
Wrong:   timeout_occurs -> Wait[1] -> reset_arm -> Stop;

8. RTS Timing operators (official)
- Wait[t]: delay exactly t. Also Wait[t1, t2] for bounded wait.
- P timeout[t] Q: P for t units, then Q via silent tau.
- P interrupt[t] Q: P runs, Q takes over after t.
- P deadline[t]: P must terminate within t.
- P within[t]: first event within t. Also within[t1, t2] for bounded range.
- Timed values: #define constants or process params ONLY (not global var).

9. Assertions
- #assert System deadlockfree;
- #assert System reaches goal; (goal must be #define proposition)
- #assert System |= []mutex; (LTL syntax, no X operator in RTS)
- #assert System |= []<>progress; (LTL liveness)

10. Conditional choices
- if (cond) {P} else {Q}: classic if-then-else, time may elapse before check.
- ifa (cond) {P} else {Q}: atomic check+execute, NO timed process in P.
- ifb (cond) {P}: blocking, waits until cond true, allowed with timed P.

11. CRITICAL RTS constraint
timeout/deadline/within/interrupt/Wait MUST NOT be inside [cond]P or ifa{}. For timed protocols, use ifb or explicit tick events: P(i) = set{i} -> tick -> tick -> tick -> ([x==i] cs -> P(i) [] [x!=i] P(i));


11.5. (CRITICAL for RTS) RTS keywords CANNOT be event names
The following words are RTS reserved keywords and CANNOT be used as event names:
  timeout, deadline, within, interrupt, Wait, waituntil, tick, tock.
Correct: timeout_occurs{...} -> P
Wrong:   timeout{...} -> P  // PARSER ERROR: Invalid Symbol 'timeout'

11.6. (CRITICAL) #define macro names CANNOT conflict with event names
If you define #define card_ejected (state == EJ), you CANNOT use card_ejected as an event name.
Correct: #define done (state == DONE); done_event{...} -> P
Wrong:   #define done (state == DONE); done{...} -> P  // "already defined as macro"

11.7. (CRITICAL for RTS) event{data} -> [guard] event is NOT supported
In RTS, a data operation event followed by a guarded event is a syntax error.
Correct: event{data} -> ifb(cond) { guarded_event -> P }
     OR: [cond] event{data} -> P  (guard BEFORE data op)
Wrong:  event{data} -> [cond] next_event -> P  // PARSER ERROR: Invalid Symbol '['

11.8. (CRITICAL for RTS) RTS ONLY supports || and ||| -- NOT alphabetized parallel
RTS does NOT support |[A]|, ||{A}||, or [|{|A|}|].
Correct: System = P || Q;          // full synchronization
Correct: System = P ||| Q;         // interleaving (no sync)
Wrong:   System = P |[a]| Q;      // NOT supported in RTS
Wrong:   System = P [|{|a|}|] Q;  // NOT supported in RTS

11.9. (CRITICAL for RTS) bounded Wait[t1,t2] and within[t1,t2] cause NullReferenceException
Despite being in the official grammar, PAT 3.5.1 crashes on bounded forms.
Use single-value forms instead:
Correct: Wait[5]; P
Wrong:   Wait[5,10]; P  // NullReferenceException in PAT 3.5.1

11.10. (CRITICAL) -> inside #define is a CSP process operator, NOT logical implication
In CSP#, -> inside #define is parsed as event prefix, NOT implication.
For safety properties, use && and ! instead:
Correct: #define safe !(train==ON_BRIDGE && gate==UP);
Wrong:   #define safe (train==ON_BRIDGE -> gate==DOWN);  // PARSER ERROR: mismatched input '->'

12. General
- Output ONLY pure PAT code — no markdown, no comments outside the code.
- Use standard CSP# variable declarations: var x = 0;
- Seq composition: P; Q (Q starts immediately after P finishes).
